channel.title: BloxWeaver Insights Blog
channel.description: Get the latest updates from BloxWeaver Insights as they happen.

item.title: BloxWeaver Pipes x Asana Connector
item.description: Introducing the BloxWeaver Pipes Asana Connector for automating project management and content workflows in Asana
item.content:encoded:
BloxWeaver Pipes x Asana Connector

We were delighted to announce the availability of our Asana connector for BloxWeaver Pipes. The connector gives you deep integration with your Asana workspaces – projects, tasks, sections, tags, comments, and attachments. Trigger a pipe when tasks move between sections or tags change – so your automations respond as work progresses. Manage content workflows in Asana. When a task moves to "Ready for Translation", automatically extract attachments or follow links, translate via AI, route to brand experts for review, then upload the localised versions and notify your team. Or sync content across systems: when a brief is approved in Asana, create a content blox in BloxWeaver, refine and localize it, push it to your CMS, and update the task with links to each language version. A wide range of actions covering tasks, projects, sections, tags, and more. If your work lives in Asana, Pipes can run it. Ready to eliminate...

Read the full article on BloxWeaver Insights →
item.author: BloxWeaver Team

item.title: BloxWeaver Pipes x Jira Connector
item.description: Introducing the BloxWeaver Pipes Jira Connector for automating issue tracking and AI-enabled agile workflows in Jira Cloud
item.content:encoded:
BloxWeaver Pipes x Jira Connector

We were delighted to announce the availability of our Atlassian Jira connector for BloxWeaver Pipes. The connector gives you deep integration with Jira Cloud – issues, comments, attachments, projects, sprints, epics, and boards. Webhook triggers fire when issues are created, updated, or change status – so your automations respond in real time. Manage multilingual content directly in Jira. When a content ticket is marked complete, automatically translate it across your target locales, route to brand experts for review, then publish to your CMS. Building features in sprints? When a story moves to "Done", trigger translation of UI strings and documentation so localisation keeps pace with development. Or trigger a Loom Agent when a feature ticket is created to collaboratively refine the specification, generate implementation code, and raise a PR in GitHub – all from a single Jira transition. 50+ actions covering issues, comments, worklogs, sprints, epics, and more. If your...

Read the full article on BloxWeaver Insights →
item.author: BloxWeaver Team

item.title: BloxWeaver Pipes x Contentstack Connector
item.description: Introducing the BloxWeaver Pipes Contentstack Connector for automating content management and translation in Contentstack cCMS
item.content:encoded:
BloxWeaver Pipes x Contentstack Connector

We were delighted to announce the availability of our Contentstack connector for BloxWeaver Pipes. The connector gives you deep integration with your Contentstack headless CMS – entries, assets, content types, locales, and workflows. Webhook triggers fire when entries are published, updated, or workflow stages change, so your automations respond in real time. Publish an entry and automatically translate it across your configured locales, route it to brand experts for review, then push the approved content back to Contentstack. Trigger an AI agent to analyse the entry and generate localised imagery for each market, tailored to regional preferences. If your content lives in Contentstack, Pipes can run it. Ready to eliminate manual work and automate your workflows? Book a demo today and see how BloxWeaver can transform your processes.

Read the full article on BloxWeaver Insights →
item.author: BloxWeaver Team

item.title: BloxWeaver Pipes x HubSpot Connector
item.description: Introducing the BloxWeaver Pipes HubSpot Connector for automating content management and translation in HubSpot CMS
item.content:encoded:
BloxWeaver Pipes x HubSpot Connector

We were delighted to announce the availability of our HubSpot CMS connector for BloxWeaver Pipes. The connector gives you deep integration with HubSpot CMS, with support for landing pages, site pages, blog posts, marketing emails, forms, and HubDB tables. Broad support unlocks a range of content automation scenarios: Publish a blog post in HubSpot and automatically generate a LinkedIn post, a newsletter snippet, and an internal Slack notification – all from a single trigger. Or pull product updates from your PIM, transform them with an AI agent, and push them straight to your landing pages. Working globally? Translate via AI and send to your brand experts for review, then push the results back to HubSpot, with variants linked automatically. With over 70 actions covering the full HubSpot CMS API. Whether you're repurposing, syncing, translating, or automating – if your content lives in HubSpot, Pipes can run it. Ready to eliminate...

Read the full article on BloxWeaver Insights →
item.author: BloxWeaver Team

item.title: BloxWeaver Pipes x Shopify Connector
item.description: Introducing the BloxWeaver Pipes Shopify Connector for automating content management and translation in your Shopify Store
item.content:encoded:
BloxWeaver Pipes x Shopify Connector

We were delighted to announce the availability of our Shopify connector for BloxWeaver Pipes. The connector gives you deep integration with your Shopify store for products, collections, pages, blogs, articles, and metafields. Add or update a product and automatically translate it via AI, route to brand experts for review, then push localised versions to your target markets - with a Slack or Teams notification when it's ready to publish. Or automate content workflows - when a new product is added, notify your global marketing teams with a draft social post and blog snippet for each market. Selling via Shopify globally? Keep your translated storefronts in sync without the manual overhead. If your content lives in Shopify, Pipes can run it. Ready to eliminate manual work and automate your workflows? Book a demo today and see how BloxWeaver can transform your processes.

Read the full article on BloxWeaver Insights →
item.author: BloxWeaver Team

item.title: BloxWeaver Pipes x Contentful Connector
item.description: Introducing the BloxWeaver Pipes Contentful Connector for automating content management and translation in Contentful cCMS
item.content:encoded:
BloxWeaver Pipes x Contentful Connector

We were delighted to announce the availability of our Contentful connector for BloxWeaver Pipes. The connector gives you deep integration with your Contentful headless CMS for your entries, assets, content types, locales, and tags. Webhook triggers fire when entries or assets are published, saved, or deleted, so your automations respond in real time. Publish an entry in Contentful and automatically translate it across your configured locales, route it to brand experts for review, then push the approved content back. Use tags to control key workflows. For example, tag an entry for review and trigger an AI agent to check tone, terminology, and brand consistency before it goes live. If your content lives in Contentful, Pipes can run it. Ready to eliminate manual work and automate your workflows? Book a demo today and see how BloxWeaver can transform your processes.

Read the full article on BloxWeaver Insights →
item.author: BloxWeaver Team

item.title: BloxWeaver Pipes x WordPress Connector
item.description: Introducing the BloxWeaver Pipes WordPress (and Polylang) Connector for automating WordPress Sites
item.content:encoded:
BloxWeaver Pipes x WordPress Connector

We were delighted to announce the availability of our WordPress connector for BloxWeaver Pipes. The connector gives you deep integration with your site - posts, pages, media, categories, tags, strings, and users - with Polylang support for multilingual workflows and enhanced multilingual string support through bloxweaver/polylang-rest-strings. Trigger a pipe when content is created or updated. Export the content, translate it through BloxWeaver, then create or update the translated post in WordPress. All automatically linked to the original. Let Pipes handle the complexity: keeping content fresh across languages, linking posts, and preserving your content and UI structure. Over 50 actions covering the full WordPress and Polylang API. If your content lives in WordPress, now your global content process can run itself. Ready to eliminate manual work and automate your workflows? Book a demo today and see how BloxWeaver can transform your processes.

Read the full article on BloxWeaver Insights →
item.author: BloxWeaver Team

item.title: BloxWeaver Pipes x GitHub Connector
item.description: Introducing the BloxWeaver Pipes GitHub Connector for automating your repos
item.content:encoded:
BloxWeaver Pipes x GitHub Connector

We were delighted to announce the availability of our GitHub connector for BloxWeaver Pipes. The connector gives you deep integration with your repositories, including branches, PRs, releases, files, and webhooks. Trigger a pipe when files are pushed to your repo. Download the changes, run them through translation or an AI agent, then commit the results back and open a PR - all automatically. When coupled with Loom Agents you can take advantage of built-in tooling to streamline app localization, or enhance your code and content review. Actions include creating branches, merging PRs, uploading files, triggering GitHub Actions workflows, and more. Webhook triggers cover push events, PR lifecycle, and releases. If your content lives in code, now your automation can too. Ready to eliminate manual work and automate your workflows? Book a demo today and see how BloxWeaver can transform your processes.

Read the full article on BloxWeaver Insights →
item.author: BloxWeaver Team

item.title: Introducing the BloxWeaver File Engineering Agent
item.description: Say goodbye to configuration bottlenecks. Our new AI agent generates file filter configurations instantly, letting localization teams move faster than ever.
item.content:encoded:
Introducing the BloxWeaver File Engineering Agent

Every localization manager knows the frustration. A new file format lands on your desk—maybe it's a complex JSON with nested HTML, an Excel sheet with specific metadata requirements, or some proprietary format your development team just started using. The translation work stops. Everything waits while you manually configure file filters or, worse, submit a support ticket and wait days for your technical team to set things up. We built the BloxWeaver File Engineering Agent to end this bottleneck and hand the power back to you and your team. <div class="relative"> <div class="wistia_responsive_padding" style="padding:56.25% 0 0 0;position:relative;"> <div class="wistia_responsive_wrapper" style="height:100%;left:0;position:absolute;top:0;width:100%;"> <div class="wistia_embed wistia_async_y7n0exz31g seo=true videoFoam=true" style="height:100%;position:relative;width:100%"> <div class="wistia_swatch" style="height:100%;left:0;opacity:0;overflow:hidden;position:absolute;top:0;transition:opacity 200ms;width:100%;"> <img src="https://fast.wistia.com/embed/medias/y7n0exz31g/swatch" style="filter:blur(5px);height:100%;object-fit:contain;width:100%;" alt="" aria-hidden="true" onload="this.parentNode.style.opacity=1;" /> </div> </div> </div> </div> <script src="https://fast.wistia.com/embed/medias/y7n0exz31g.jsonp" async></script> <script src="https://fast.wistia.com/assets/external/E-v1.js" async></script> </div> The Problem: Configuration Complexity Slows Teams Down In today's fast-moving environment, the content types you need to support evolve constantly. New systems are...

Read the full article on BloxWeaver Insights →
item.author: BloxWeaver Team

item.title: Discussing Leadership and AI on MBN's A Class Act Podcast
item.description: Our founder David Meikle joins Shannon McKechnie on MBN Solution's A Class Act podcast for a wide-ranging conversation covering Leadership and Artificial Intelligence.
item.content:encoded:
Discussing Leadership and AI on MBN's A Class Act Podcast

We were delighted to join Shannon McKechnie on MBN Solution's podcast, A Class Act: Conversations in Data and Leadership. On the podcast our founder, David Meikle, reflects on the leadership lessons he learned introducing AI and automation into the translation industry nearly ten years before most businesses began facing the same challenges. It was a wide-ranging conversation covering the following main topics: How to lead teams through fear of AI and show its value. Why psychological safety is essential for innovation and high performance. How David rebuilt trust after leading a restructure early in his career. The leadership principles behind scaling from 12% to 98% online orders in a year. Why patience, adaptability, and strengths-based leadership matter most in change. You can find the episode on: Spotify Apple Podcasts YouTube

Read the full article on BloxWeaver Insights →
item.author: David Meikle

item.title: BloxWeaver Featured in Slator's Language AI 50 Under 50
item.description: We're thrilled to share that BloxWeaver has been included in Slator's 2025 Language AI 50 Under 50 list, showcasing fifty of the most innovative language AI companies founded in the last 50 months.
item.content:encoded:
BloxWeaver Featured in Slator's Language AI 50 Under 50

We're thrilled to share that BloxWeaver has been included in Slator's 2025 Language AI 50 Under 50, showcasing fifty of the most innovative and fast-growing language AI companies founded within the last 50 months. Slator, the leading industry publication covering language services and technology, selected BloxWeaver as part of their 2025 cohort highlighting companies that are "showing increasing differentiation" and "emerging as vertical specialists, moving beyond core language functionalities to deliver 'whole product' experiences." It specifically mentions BloxWeaver's mission to "eliminate localization friction with end-to-end content services that support multimodal formats, model fine-tuning, and workflow agents." This recognition comes at an exciting time for the language AI industry. As Slator notes, this year's startups "couldn't be further from generic translation tools or prompt-based LLM outputs. Instead of one-size-fits-all text translation and generation, they offer structured control, integrated workflows, and business-specific capabilities." What a journey it's been since launching last year,...

Read the full article on BloxWeaver Insights →
item.author: David Meikle

item.title: The Precision Content Series: Context Enrichment
item.description: Explore Context Enrichment - the intelligence layer that transforms good translations into great ones. Learn how rich contextual information enables nuanced decision-making in precision content localization.
item.content:encoded:
The Precision Content Series: Context Enrichment

Welcome back to The Precision Content Series, where we explore the essential pillars that enable organizations to deliver precision content at global scale. In our previous posts, we introduced the concept of precision content and examined the critical foundation of Content Processing and Filtering. Today, we turn our attention to what many consider the most transformative pillar: Context Enrichment. Context Enrichment represents the difference between good and great translation. It involves identifying and leveraging appropriate contextual information - whether actively integrated into translation production or provided as reference material to guide human translators and AI models toward more accurate, culturally appropriate outcomes. Building on the content processing capabilities we explored previously, context enrichment extends far beyond the immediate content being translated, drawing from multiple sources to create a rich understanding that enables truly precision localization. This includes dynamic enrichment techniques that leverage relationships within content, external knowledge sources, and even...

Read the full article on BloxWeaver Insights →
item.author: David Meikle

item.title: The Precision Content Series: Content Processing and Filtering
item.description: Explore the often unsung hero of quality localisation: Content Processing and Filtering. Learn how what happens before your content reaches translators can dramatically impact quality, efficiency, and cost.
item.content:encoded:
The Precision Content Series: Content Processing and Filtering

Welcome back to The Precision Content Series - our focus on the key areas that help deliver precision content. In our previous post, we introduced what we mean by precision content and outlined the five pillars required to achieve it: Content Processing and Filtering Context Enrichment Content Workbench Model Selection and Optimization Subject Matter Expertise and Review Today, we spotlight the often unsung hero of quality localisation: Content Processing and Filtering. While most organizations often focus much of their effort on translation mechanics, what happens before your content reaches translators or AI systems can dramatically impact quality, efficiency, and cost. This crucial first step involves deciding what content to include or exclude for translation, identifying or generating additional context, and determining what adaptations are required before or after translation. Why Content Processing Matters Consider the scenario, a global e-commerce company needs to translate its product catalog into 36 languages. At...

Read the full article on BloxWeaver Insights →
item.author: David Meikle

item.title: The Precision Content Series: What is Precision Content?
item.description: An introduction to precision content and why high-value materials require meticulous attention to linguistic nuance, cultural context, and technical accuracy during translation and localization.
item.content:encoded:
The Precision Content Series: What is Precision Content?

Over the past ten years, content creation and distribution have reached unprecedented scales. This expansion, driven by the increasing global nature of modern business, has brought both new opportunities and significant challenges in maintaining quality across languages and cultures. The rise of AI technologies, particularly in translation and localization, has created a complex landscape of possibilities and pitfalls. While AI has been part of the translation industry longer than most - with the original Transformer model designed specifically for machine translation - today's environment of heightened AI awareness has created new dynamics. Many vendors have leaned into the current AI hype cycle, emphasising large language models and automated solutions that promise quick, cheap translations. However, for organisations with high-value content, this approach often falls short of delivering the required quality and precision. For organisations with high-value content, we need to think differently. We define 'precision content' as materials that require...

Read the full article on BloxWeaver Insights →
item.author: David Meikle

item.title: AI-Driven Content Management and Multilingual Strategies on The Lucent Perspective
item.description: Our founder David Meikle joins Rebecca Hastings on The Lucent Perspective podcast for a wide-ranging conversation covering content management, AI customisation, and preparing companies for exits.
item.content:encoded:
AI-Driven Content Management and Multilingual Strategies on The Lucent Perspective

We were delighted to join Rebecca Hastings on her podcast, The Lucent Perspective for a wide ranging conversation covering content management, AI customisation, and preparing companies for exits. On the podcast our founder, David Meikle, shared his expertise on leveraging AI to tackle multilingual content management, a challenge many global companies face today. He shared a deep dive into "content fabric" and how it is reshaping enterprise content strategies. It was a wide ranging conversation covering the following main topics: Challenges in multilingual content management AI applications in language technology The concept of "content fabric" for global businesses Customizing AI for enterprise success Benefits of open-source contributions to career development Preparing companies for exits from a CTO's perspective Give it a listen if you're curious about how AI is shaping the future of content, and let us know your thoughts. Thanks again to Rebecca for the great conversation! You can...

Read the full article on BloxWeaver Insights →
item.author: David Meikle
